item.title: Proclamation by the President on National Angel Family Day, 2026
item.description: This presidential proclamation designates National Angel Family Day for February 2026. It memorializes individuals who have died as a result of crimes involving individuals who entered the United States unlawfully and acknowledges the harms associated with illicit drug trafficking across the border. The document references families affected by these losses, known as Angel Families, and expresses the President's intent to observe the day, encourage remembrance and public awareness, and reflect on related public safety and border-security concerns. It calls on federal, state, local governments, organizations, and the public to observe the day and to consider efforts aimed at addressing the factors contributing to such deaths. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Nominations Sent to the Senate for U.S. Attorney for the District of Nevada and for Assistant Secretaries
item.description: The White House announced that nominations have been sent to the Senate for several federal positions. The nominees named include George Kelesis to be United States Attorney for the District of Nevada for a four-year term, replacing Jason M. Frierson; Catherine Dillon to be Assistant Secretary of State for Educational and Cultural Affairs, replacing Lee Satterfield; and William Hague to be Assistant Secretary of the Interior. The announcements reflect a list of forthcoming confirmation actions on the White House’s official nominations page. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Strengthening United States National Defense by Enhancing Electric Grid Reliability through Clean Coal Power Generation
item.description: Objective of this executive action is to ensure the United States electric grid, which supports military installations, operations, and defense-industrial production, remains resilient and reliable and not dependent on intermittent energy sources. The action emphasizes that the grid underpins national defense and economic stability and outlines measures to prevent prolonged disruptions caused by energy supply issues, including the use of clean coal power generation capacity to bolster grid resilience. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.
